LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS LAI-SAU CHAN LEE AND TONY MING FAI LEE'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT OF PLAINTIFF SUSAN Y F LEE IS SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE SEVENTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR CONSPIRACY AND AS TO THE NINTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENCE. SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE EIGHTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR WRONGFUL EVICTION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077
CASE MANAGEMENT STATEMENT FILED BY DEFENDANT LEE, TONY MING FAI LEE, LAI-SAU CHAN ESTIMATED TIME FOR TRIAL: 5.0 DAYS

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS TONY LEE, LAI-SAU LEE'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED IN ITS ENTIRETY. PLAINTIFF'S CONSPIRACY ALLEGATIONS ARE SUFFICIENT. READING THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT AS A WHOLE, PLAINTIFF ALLEGES THAT BOTH TONY LEE AND LAI-SAU LEE WERE PART OF THE CONSPIRACY AND THEY CAN BE LIABLE FOR THE ACTS THAT WAH-BUN LEE ALLEGEDLY TOOK TO FURTHER THE CONSPIRACY. THE COURT REJECTS THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS ARGUMENT BECAUSE EVEN IF SOME OF THE ACTS ARE TIME BARRED, DEFENDANTS FAILED TO SHOW THAT THE CAUSES OF ACTION ARE BARRED IN THEIR ENTIRETY. PLAINTIFF SUFFICIENTLY PLEADS A LANDLORD TENANT RELATIONSHIP WITH MS. LAI-SAU LEE. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA MILLER, COURT REPORTER: TEANNA WARD, CSR #11918

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT WAH-BUN LEE'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED IN ITS ENTIRETY. THE 1ST THROUGH 6TH CAUSES OF ACTION ARE NOT UNCERTAIN. PLAINTIFF SUFFICIENTLY PLEADS THE EXISTENCE OF A CONSPIRACY AND MR. WAH-BUN LEE IS LIABLE FOR THE ACTS THAT HE TOOK TO FURTHER THE CONSPIRACY. THERE IS NO BASIS TO STRIKE THE CONSPIRACY ALLEGATIONS AND THE MOTION TO STRIKE IS DENIED. THE DEMURRER TO THE 7TH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENCE IS ALSO OVERRULED; PLAINTIFF'S ALLEGATIONS THAT MR. WAH-BUN LEE IS A LANDLORD ARE SUFFICIENT, AND IT STATES A CAUSE OF ACTION. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA MILLER, COURT REPORTER: TEANNA WARD, CSR #11918

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: JUDGE MILLER DISCLOSED THAT SHE KNOWS ELLEN LIU, A FORMER RESEARCH ATTORNEY FOR THE COURT. THE COURT DID NOT WORK WITH MS. LIU IN THIS CASE. THESE FACTS DO NOT AFFECT THE COURT'S ABILITY TO BE FAIR AND IMPARTIAL IN THIS CASE. COURT GAVE COUNSEL THE OPPORTUNITY TO ASK QUESTIONS. NO QUESTIONS WERE ASKED OR OBJECTIONS WERE RAISED. THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENTS, THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS WAH-BUN LEE, TONY LEE AND LAI-SAU LEE'S MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF'S INDEPENDENT MENTAL EXAMINATON IS GRANTED FOR REASONS STATED ON THE RECORD. THE EXAMINATION IS TO PROCEED ON FEBRUARY 7, 2013 AND SHALL UTILIZE A CANTONESE INTERPRETER. ORDER SIGNED. (SEE ORDER FOR DETAILS OF THE RULING.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: TERI DARNELL, CSR # 9966.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT WAH-BUN LEE'S MOTION TO STAY PROCEEDINGS IS GRANTED IN PART. THE DEPOSITION OF WAH-BUN LEE MAY NOT OCCUR UNTIL THE LATER OF JANUARY 1, 2013 OR THE CONCLUSION OF ANY CRIMINAL PROCEEDINGS AGAINST MR. LEE BASED ON THE SAME ALLEGATIONS AS SET FORTH IN THE COMPLAINT FILED BY THE PLAINTIFF. NO OTHER PORTION OF THE CASE IS STAYED. HOWEVER, MR. LEE IS NOT PRECLUDED FROM ASSERTING IN GOOD FAITH THE FIFTH AMENDMENT AS TO ANY WRITTEN DISCOVERY SERVED ON HIM. IT IS EXPECTED THAT, IF NO CRIMINAL CASE IS BROUGHT AGAINST MR. LEE BY DECEMBER 31, 2012, THIS CASE SHOULD BE IN A POSITION TO BE TRIED IN EARLY 2013 SHORTLY AFTER MR. LEE'S DEPOSITION WILL HAVE TAKEN PLACE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE (TRIAL COURT)
Off Calendar Jul-18-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearance by Michael Mutalipassi, Esq. for Plaintiff Susan Y. F. Lee
Appearance by Melvin Honowitz, Esq. for Defendant Wah-Bun Lee
Constance Yu, Esq. for Defendants Lai-Sau Chan Lee and Tony Ming Lee
Cantonese Interpreter for Plaintiff: Gini Leong

This matter comes on calendar this day for the parties to report on the status of their settlement agreement. The parties confirmed that the entire case has settled pursuant to Plaintiff's acceptance of Defendants' CCP 998 offer. Terms of settlement read into the record as follows:

Defendants Wah-Bun Lee, Lai-Sau Chan Lee and Tony Ming Lee agree to pay Plaintiff Susan Y F Lee the sum of $75,000.00 in exchange for a dismissal with prejudice of all causes of action. Each party shall bear his and her own attorney's fees and costs. Parties agreed to terms on the record.

Further terms of the settlement is as reflected on the court reporter's transcript.

Judge: CYNTHIA M. LEE; Clerk: JIROQUE; Reporter: Pamela R. Katros, CSR# 9383, Tel. No. (916) 879-0406. (206)

MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Melvin D. Honowitz, Esq. of the Law Offices of Melvin Honowitz & Associates, Tel. 415.684.9462, for defendants and moving party;

Michael Mutalipassi, Esq. of Wong & Associates, Tel. 415.397.2622, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: Judge Miller disclosed that she knows Ellen Liu, a former research attorney for the court. The Court did not work with Ms. Liu in this case. These facts do not affect the Court's ability to be fair and impartial in this case. Court gave counsel the opportunity to ask questions. No questions were asked or objections were raised. The matter was argued. At the conclusion of arguments, the court ruled as follows: DEFENDANTS WAH-BUN LEE, TONY LEE and LAI-SAU LEE'S Motion To Compel Plaintiff's Independent Mental Examinaton is granted for reasons stated on the record. The examination is to proceed on February 7, 2013 and shall utilize a Cantonese interpreter. Order signed. (See order for details of the ruling.) Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Teri Darnell, CSR # 9966.

Joinder In Req. For An Order To Compel Pltf'S Independent Mental Examination And For An Order To Compel Production Fo Moca Raw Data
Off Calendar Dec-05-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael Mutalipassi, Esq., 415.397.2622, for plaintiff Susan Y.F. Lee.
Melvin D. Honowitz, Esq., 415.684.9462, for defendant Wah-Bun Lee.
Ellen P. Liu, Esq., 415.392.1960, for defendants Lai-Sau Chan Lee and Tony Ming Lee.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: defendants Lai-Sau Chan Lee and Tony Ming Lee's motion for an order to compel plaintiff's independent mental examination and the production for moca raw data test results and DEFENDANT WAH-BUN LEE'S Joinder is denied without prejudice. The Court denies the request for an independent mental examination because defendants do not provide sufficient information regarding the proposed examination. Given the lack of information in this motion, the Court cannot issue an order in compliance with CCP 2032.320(d). The Court denies the request for the MOCA data. Defendants should comply with the production request procedure. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: A. James Robertson II, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-19-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT WAH-BUN LEE's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.
The demurrer is overruled in its entirety. The 1st through 6th causes of action are not uncertain. Plaintiff sufficiently pleads the existence of a conspiracy and Mr. Wah-Bun Lee is liable for the acts that he took to further the conspiracy. There is no basis to strike the conspiracy allegations and the motion to strike is denied. The demurrer to the 7th cause of action for negligence is also overruled; plaintiff's allegations that Mr. Wah-Bun Lee is a landlord are sufficient, and it states a cause of action. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla Miller, Court Reporter: Teanna Ward, CSR #11918

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-19-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANTS TONY LEE AND LAI-SAU LEE'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is denied. Plaintiff's allegations are sufficient to support a claim for punitive damages. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla Miller, Court Reporter: Teanna Ward, CSR #11918

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-19-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANTs TONY LEE, LAI-SAU LEE's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.
The demurrer is overruled in its entirety. Plaintiff's conspiracy allegations are sufficient. Reading the First Amended Complaint as a whole, plaintiff alleges that both Tony Lee and Lai-Sau Lee were part of the conspiracy and they can be liable for the acts that Wah-Bun Lee allegedly took to further the conspiracy. The Court rejects the statute of limitations argument because even if some of the acts are time barred, defendants failed to show that the causes of action are barred in their entirety. Plaintiff sufficiently pleads a landlord tenant relationship with Ms. Lai-Sau Lee. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla Miller, Court Reporter: Teanna Ward, CSR #11918

Ntc Of Motion And Motion To Stay Proceedings
Off Calendar Oct-31-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT WAH-BUN LEE'S MOTION TO STAY PROCEEDINGS.

APPEARANCES: Michael Mutalipassi, Esq. of Wong & Associates 397-2622 for pltf & opposing party; Constance J. Yu, Esq. of Sideman & Bancroft LLP 733-3904 for defts Lai-Sau Chan Lee And Tony Ming Fai Lee; Melvin D. Honowitz, Esq. of Law Office of Melvin D. Honowitz & Associates 684-9462 for deft Wah-Bun Lee.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling - Granted in part. The deposition of Wah-Bun Lee may not occur until the later of January 1, 2013 or the conclusion of any criminal proceedings against Mr. Lee based on the same allegations as set forth in the complaint filed by the plaintiff. No other portion of the case is stayed. However, Mr. Lee is not precluded from asserting in good faith the Fifth Amendment as to any written discovery served on him. It is expected that, if no criminal case is brought against Mr. Lee by December 31, 2012, this case should be in a position to be tried in early 2013 shortly after Mr. Lee's deposition will have taken place. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077
